prompt_rendered:
You are writing the body paragraph below the hero headline on a Brain Diff
results page.

The headline has just landed a sharp claim drawn from the strongest coupling
pattern in this comparison. The body paragraph's job: SUPPORT THE HEADLINE
in plain language — explain WHY the cortex behaved this way, ground it in
what the videos actually did, and HOOK the reader to keep reading.

Do NOT pad with "different strategies, neither is winning" filler. That's
generic — the user has already understood the page is a strategy comparison
from the headline. Your job is to convince them the contrast is REAL and
worth scrolling for.

Format: 2-3 sentences, ≤ 60 words. Direct claims, no hedge language. Use
the video TITLES (no creator names). No anatomical jargon, no percentages,
no citations, no year stamps, no winner/loser framing.

Inputs:
- The headline (already chosen, must be supported): Cleo Abram's thinking sparks a deeper connection.
MrBeast's emotions ignite a more intellectual response.
- Video A title: Cleo Abram
- Video B title: MrBeast
- The lead insight that drove the headline:
  - In Cleo Abram, Memory encoding and Language rose together across the runtime (r = +0.65) — these two systems fired as a team.
- Top 2 system deltas with direction:
  - memory_encoding: -0.34 (A higher)
  - language_depth: -0.31 (A higher)
- Recipe A name + short description: Slow Build → Deep Encode — Gradual attention building over the runtime, deep language network engagement, memory encoding throughout, climax in a Reasoning Beat in the
- Recipe B name + short description: Visceral Hook → Low-Effort Hold — Immediate anterior insula spike in the opening seconds, sustained low cognitive effort across the middle, late Emotional Impact in the closi

Voice exemplars (study carefully — these are the body anchors):
  1. Two videos that look similar in the wild and feel different to watch. The cortex shows why. Each runs a distinct cognitive recipe — different systems pulled, different timing, different cortical fingerprints. Neither is winning. They're playing different games.
  2. Same length, same upload week, same thumbnail energy. The brain receives them as opposites. MrBeast pulls the body before the mind catches up. Cleo builds the mind without ever asking the body. Different objectives, different machinery.
  3. Both videos open with what creators call hooks. The cortex disagrees about what 'hook' means. One arrives in the gut at second eight. The other arrives in the language network at second twenty. They're optimizing for different cortical outcomes.
  4. The cortex doesn't grade content. It runs whatever is presented through whatever systems are necessary. MrBeast presents stakes; the anterior insula responds. Cleo presents claims; the language and memory networks respond. Two videos, two recruitment patterns, no contest.

Generate 3 candidate body paragraphs. Select the one that most clearly
supports the headline AND ends with a sentence that pulls the reader into
the rest of the page. Output only the selected paragraph as a single string.
No explanation.
candidates: Cleo Abram's thinking sparks a deeper connection because her videos expertly combine memory encoding and language, firing these two systems in perfect harmony. This synergy is evident in the way her content builds gradually, engaging the language network and encoding memories throughout, culminating in a Reasoning Beat that leaves a lasting impact. As you watch, you'll see how this unique approach sets her apart from others, and we'll dive deeper into the science behind her success.
selected: Cleo Abram's thinking sparks a deeper connection because her videos expertly combine memory encoding and language, firing these two systems in perfect harmony. This synergy is evident in the way her content builds gradually, engaging the language network and encoding memories throughout, culminating in a Reasoning Beat that leaves a lasting impact. As you watch, you'll see how this unique approach sets her apart from others, and we'll dive deeper into the science behind her success.
